title: Repair Heim-PC Node runtime JIT crash
goal: Diagnose and repair the host-level Node/V8 JIT crash that makes `node -e`, `npm`, `npx`, `pnpm` and the npm Codex entrypoint abort with rc 133 unless `NODE_OPTIONS=--jitless` is used.

acceptance.assertion: `node -e 'console.log(1)'`, `npm --version`, `npx --version` and `pnpm --version` complete without rc 133 in a normal shell, or a precise upstream/runtime blocker is recorded.
The fix does not globally force `NODE_OPTIONS=--jitless` unless the trade-off is explicitly accepted and documented.
Codex wrapper behavior remains green: `codex --version` resolves to the intended standalone Codex binary or a documented fallback.

metadata.live_audit_finding: `codex --version` crashed through the shell PTY wrapper because `~/bin/codexi` prepended `~/.npm-global/bin` and invoked the npm Codex entrypoint; direct `~/.local/bin/codex --version` returned codex-cli 0.142.2, while normal Node/npm still abort with a V8 SetPermissions fatal error and rc 133.

validated_acceptance.operator-pc-hygiene-v1-t005-1: The active Git-bound wrapper release passed 50 fresh node -e processes and 10 each npm, npx and pnpm probes, plus an npm child process, without rc 133.
validated_acceptance.operator-pc-hygiene-v1-t005-2: The source receipt records globally_forces_jitless=false. Only MemoryDenyWriteExecute is relaxed in transient user units while NoNewPrivileges remains 1.
validated_acceptance.operator-pc-hygiene-v1-t005-3: Standalone ~/.local/bin/codex remained unchanged and returned codex-cli 0.142.2 in final postflight.

validation: PRs #117 and #118 passed full Infra CI and local make validate on their final exact heads.
PR #117 live target mismatch was stopped and all three previous wrappers were restored byte-for-byte.
PR #118 test-harness ERR-trap rollback restored the previous wrappers before the final clean activation.
Final activation is source-bound to merge 0fc7a0f30ec47aaf84774e3721e514c86059774e.
Final postflight preserved environment values and PTY semantics, retained NoNewPrivileges=1, left no environment files and kept standalone Codex green.
metadata.outcome: Node, npm, npx and pnpm are green through a versioned, Git-bound and reversible transient-unit boundary. The Grabowski service hardening remains unchanged; only MemoryDenyWriteExecute is relaxed for the three fixed Node-family targets.
metadata.self_review: PASS: current public links, source receipt, final result and every cited local evidence hash were re-read after activation. Failed rollout and test-harness rollback events remain explicitly documented rather than smoothed over.
